title: Teaching
text: I teach at the Complutense University of Madrid in the Department of Social, Work and Differential Psychology.

title: Teaching
text:
Current Courses

Individual Differences
Undergraduate — Faculty of Psychology, Complutense University of Madrid
This course provides an introduction to the scientific study of psychological differences between individuals. Topics include the history and theoretical foundations of differential psychology, psychometric methods, intelligence and cognitive abilities, personality, and the interplay of genetic and environmental factors.







Note



Teaching materials and course resources will be added here.

title: Scholarly Communication
text:
The current academic publishing system — dominated by a small number of commercial publishers — is costly, restrictive, and misaligned with the interests of science and society. This research line develops theoretical models and practical tools to support a transition toward scholar-governed, open-access publishing.
In 2012, I co-founded Open Scholar, a non-profit initiative dedicated to reforming academic publishing. Our work has addressed peer review reform, overlay journals, institutional repositories, and the political economy of open access.
 OSF Project  GitHub

title: Exercise, Brain & Cognition
text:
Does physical exercise improve cognitive performance? If so, how? This research line uses neuroimaging (EEG), psychophysiological measures, and rigorous experimental designs to investigate the acute and chronic effects of exercise on brain function and cognition — with a focus on sustained attention and cardiac interoception.
Key questions include: How does aerobic fitness modulate brain oscillations during demanding cognitive tasks? What is the relationship between exercise, autonomic regulation, and the perception of internal bodily signals?
 OSF Project  GitHub

                            Neuropsychologia, 198, 108867.
                            
                            doi: 10.1016/j.neuropsychologia.2024.108867
                            
                        
                        
                        Investigates whether regular aerobic exercise enhances the ability to perceive internal cardiac signals, using heartbeat detection tasks and psychophysiological measures.

title: Pandelis Perakakis
text:
Associate Professor of Psychology
      Complutense University of Madrid
      Dept. of Social, Work and Differential Psychology
      
        I am an associate professor of psychology at the Complutense University of Madrid,
        teaching Individual Differences. My research encompasses human psychophysiology and
        affective science, with a particular interest in affect shift dynamics and their
        significance for psychological well-being.
      
      
        
           ORCID
        
        
           Google Scholar
        
        
           GitHub
        
        
           X
        
        
           LinkedIn

title: Affect Dynamics
text:
Emotions are not static — they fluctuate over time, and the pattern of those fluctuations may matter as much as their average level for psychological well-being. Drawing on concepts from dynamical systems theory, this research line investigates how affect shifts over hours and days, and what those dynamics reveal about a person’s psychological health.
A key finding from recent work is that emotional experience often behaves like a bistable system: rather than drifting gradually between positive and negative states, most people tend to remain in one state before abruptly tipping into another. This bistability — the tendency to occupy distinct emotional attractors — predicts well-being above and beyond traditional affect measures.
 OSF Project  GitHub

title: Affect Dynamics
text:
Journal Articles



    
        
            
                
                
                    
                
                

                
                    
                        
                            Goicoechea, C., Dakos, V., Sanabria, D., Ciria, L. F., & Perakakis, P. (2025).
                            Bistability and affect shift dynamics in the prediction of psychological well-being.
                            Emotion, 25(4), 982–996.
                            
                            doi: 10.1037/emo0001441
                            
                        
                        
                        We apply bistability metrics from dynamical systems theory to experience-sampling data and show that the tendency to occupy distinct emotional attractors predicts well-being beyond traditional affect measures.

title: Research
text:
My research sits at the intersection of human psychophysiology, affective science, and scholarly communication. Below are the three main programmes I am currently pursuing.



Affect Dynamics
How do affect shifts over hours and days relate to psychological well-being? This programme draws on dynamical systems theory to investigate bistable emotional dynamics.
View programme →


Scholarly Communication
Developing models and tools for scholar-governed, open-access publishing. Co-founded Open Scholar in 2012.
View programme →


Exercise, Brain & Cognition
Does physical exercise improve cognition? This programme uses EEG, psychophysiology, and experimental designs to investigate exercise-brain links.
View programme →

title: Software
text: I develop open-source software tools for psychophysiological data analysis. Both tools are freely available under open licenses.

title: Software
text:
KARDIA
KARDIA (καρδιά — “heart” in Greek) is a MATLAB software for the analysis of cardiac interbeat interval data. It provides a graphical user interface with functions for examining phasic cardiac responses triggered by environmental stimuli and calculating standard heart rate variability parameters.

Features

Analysis of phasic cardiac responses to environmental stimuli
Time and frequency domain Heart Rate Variability (HRV) measures
Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (DFA) for scaling exponents
Grand average and individual subject result visualization
Data export to spreadsheets for statistical analysis
Free distribution under GNU Public License



Download

 GitHub SourceForge Zenodo



Citation
Perakakis, P., Joffily, M., Taylor, M., Guerra, P., & Vila, J. (2010). KARDIA: a Matlab software for the analysis of cardiac interbeat intervals. Computer Methods and Programs in Biomedicine, 98, 83–89.

title: Software
text:
HEPLAB
HEPLAB is an EEGLAB plugin and standalone MATLAB toolbox for the analysis of the Heartbeat-Evoked Potential (HEP). It automates the detection of cardiac-related events from raw ECG signals and integrates with EEGLAB for downstream EEG analysis.

Features

Three R wave detection algorithms
One T wave detection algorithm
Graphical user interface for displaying cardiac events and manually correcting artifacts
Export of events to EEGLAB’s EEG structure for HEP analysis
Usable as an EEGLAB plugin or standalone MATLAB toolbox



Download

 GitHub Zenodo
